articles.title: How to end a TV show
articles.description: Ending any story is hard, but that's especially true of mystery-packed TV shows. Series like Lost initially hook viewers with constantly building secrets and questions, to the point that they can often seem incomprehensible. But the promise is that it will all pay off in the end - a feat that few sh

articles.title: Ford rehires human engineers after AI fails to match quality checks
articles.description: The car-maker found AI quality checks failed to match the skill of veteran technicians.

articles.title: 'Big brother' Van Dijk key to Dutch World Cup dreams
articles.description: France's World Cup winner Olivier Giroud explains why Netherlands defender Virgil van Dijk is still the main man for his national team.

articles.title: Physicists Discover a Fundamental Limit to Electrical Resistance
articles.description: A quantum simulation found a ceiling on resistivity caused by electron collisions. Every time electricity flows through a wire, some of its energy is inevitably lost as heat because electrons collide with one another and with the material around them. articles.title: Could a heatwave make World Cup matches 'unsafe' this week?
articles.description: Temperatures are expected to soar across parts of the US and Canada this week which could bring significant health impacts to some World Cup matches.

articles.title: Comcast is splitting in two
articles.description: Comcast has announced plans to separate itself into two publicly traded companies, spinning off its NBCUniversal and Sky broadcasting arms. articles.title: Tuchel still searching for answers out wide - Shearer
articles.description: Former England captain Alan Shearer explains why there are lots of reasons to be positive about England so far at this World Cup, but having a settled side is not one of them.

articles.title: Physicists Solve a “Quantum-Only” Problem Using an Ordinary Laptop

articles.title: British American Tobacco to cut 9,000 jobs
articles.description: The company had said earlier this year it was planning savings to make it "more digital and AI-focused".

articles.description: Manie Libbok will start in place of the injured Sacha Feinberg-Mngomezulu at fly-half for South Africa against England on Saturday.

articles.title: Robin Byrd, the Sex Godmother of Millennials, Says the Internet Ruined Porn
articles.description: The 1970s porn actress turned New York City late night public access queen says censorship and a lack of star quality among modern adult entertainers turned her off of the industry.

articles.title: Yeasound RIC800 Hearing Aids Review: Good Audio, Glitchy App
articles.description: With AI-powered noise reduction, an automatic speech-focusing system, and a simple, effective hearing test, it’s a shame these aids don't come with a better app.

articles.title: More change needed after Stokes retires - Vaughan
articles.description: Former captain Michael Vaughan says he will be "absolutely staggered" if there is not more leadership change in English cricket after Ben Stokes' retirement.

articles.title: Scientists Unlock Hidden DNA From 1,300-Year-Old Manuscripts
articles.description: Historic parchments may hold genetic clues that can be studied without harming the manuscripts. articles.title: NASA's X-59 "frankenjet" tests supersonic flight without the sonic boom
articles.description: NASA’s quiet supersonic flight tests could eventually go on a national tour.

articles.title: The Anti-Data-Center Movement Is Reshaping Michigan Politics
articles.description: Climate activist Will Lawrence cofounded the Sunrise Movement. Now, he has shifted his focus in his attempt to compete for a swing-district seat by calling for a data center moratorium.

articles.description: Drone shows are the new fireworks—and possibly a new kind of religion. I traveled to Texas to be converted.

articles.description: Europe’s pro-competition proposals could see Google Search and Android systems opened up. The company claims there are serious privacy flaws.

articles.title: I'd ticked all the boxes so decision to resign easy - Clarke
articles.description: Steve Clarke says it was an easy decision to step down as Scotland head coach because he always planned to depart if they did not reach the knockout stage.

articles.title: Scientists Discover Ultrasound May Stop Arthritis Before It Starts

articles.title: Conspiracy or chaos? Algeria & Austria defend 'unexpected' draw
articles.description: The 'Disgrace of Gijon' - an infamous match at the 1982 World Cup in Spain - has followed Austria and Algeria for 44 years. Did history repeat itself in Algeria's 3-3 draw with Austria on Sunday?

articles.title: Why did this journal retract two 1940s papers by Max Planck?
articles.description: Clicking on the links now reveals blank pages and empty PDFs. "Intellectually, it’s not acceptable.”

articles.title: Scientists Discover Why the Same Volcano Erupted in Two Completely Different Ways
articles.description: Mount Etna’s past eruptions reveal that a single volcano can erupt through very different underground pathways. articles.title: China claims the world’s fastest supercomputer
articles.description: Despite trade restrictions, China has reclaimed the title of the world's fastest supercomputer for the first time since 2018.
